issue_title: t-test for non-equal distribution variances
issue_body:
Current: for t-test we assume that two populations have equal variances. The case of non-equal variances is not implemented.
Possible solution: add Welch's t-test implementation for the case of unequal populations variances.

Note: this question should be preliminary investigated: when it's necessary or helpful, when it's not.

issue_title: Convert camelcased parameter names to Python style convention
issue_body:
### Description
It's common to define Swagger parameter names in camelcase ("postalCode", "modifiedDate", "statusCode", etc.), but this goes against Python PEP-8 convention.

### Expected behaviour

From Swagger:

    get:
      parameters:
        - name: postalCode
          in: query
          type: string

I would like to be able to write

    def search(postal_code, ...):
        ...

### Actual behaviour

The parameters are not found:

    TypeError: search() missing 1 required positional argument: 'postal_code'


### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

$ python --version
Python 3.5.2
$ pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"
Version: 1.0.129


Here's a regex for the conversion: http://stackoverflow.com/a/1176023/594211

issue_title: Toggling guake scrolls window
issue_body:
Hitting F12 twice to toggle guake on and off scrolls the whole output as if a bunch of new lines would have been added (see included images).

Guake with some random output to fill the screen and show maximum effect of unexpected behaviour:
![screenshot from 2016-10-27 13-56-55](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10351100/19766695/94734bba-9c4f-11e6-86f7-5aca5174e3f5.png)
Guake after hitting F12 twice:
![screenshot from 2016-10-27 13-57-07](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10351100/19766698/97a50418-9c4f-11e6-9914-c9f0b9b9ff6c.png)

Note that hitting F12 afterwards will again scroll the screen by probably the same amount. I can't tell for sure since after hitting F12 another two times the terminal is scrolled so far that I am left with a blank screen.

Typing anything other than the keybindings F12 (toggle guake) and F11 (min/max window) however instantly "jumps" everything back to normal. This bug started appearing when I upgraded my guake version to 0.8.7 on my unbuntu 16.04 x64 machine, unfortunately I cannot remember which version I was using before. I did a `git pull `and install via `./dev.sh --install` a few minutes ago and the bug is still persistent in the current master branch. The bug also appears when toggling fullscreen via F11.

Additionally I noticed the following output when I start guake via the terminal, which might be related to this issue:
when starting guake:
> (guake:4408): libglade-WARNING **: unknown attribute `swapped' for <signal>.
> INFO     Logging configuration complete
> DEBUG    do not set background from user

and after pressing F12 twice the following lines appear:
> /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/guake/guake_app.py:884: GtkWarning: IA__gtk_window_set_type_hint: assertion '!gtk_widget_get_mapped (GTK_WIDGET (window))' failed
>   self.window.set_type_hint(gtk.gdk.WINDOW_TYPE_HINT_DOCK)
> /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/guake/guake_app.py:885: GtkWarning: IA__gtk_window_set_type_hint: assertion '!gtk_widget_get_mapped (GTK_WIDGET (window))' failed
>   self.window.set_type_hint(gtk.gdk.WINDOW_TYPE_HINT_NORMAL)
> DEBUG    do not set background from user

additional F12 toggling afterwards just reproduces the DEBUG message.

Please let me know whether this information is sufficient to tackle this issue and if this even is a bug in the current branch or just a problem on my machine due to some faulty configuration. I am happy to provide any additional info in case something is missing and would be very grateful for any help for the case that I simply misconfigured guake. Note that I haven't installed the glade-gtk2 package since it isn't in the repositories for ubuntu 16.04, but from what I figured it should not be needed for guake to work properly.

issue_title: how to set multiple options when intializing jira.JIRA object
issue_body:
Hi,

on our JIRA we have a plugin that can be accessed by REST API.
the difference in the "call" is the JIRA_BASE_URL
so e.g. instead of https://{my_jira_server}/rest/api/2/{issue_id}
I need to call https://{my_jira_server}/rest/{something_different_than_api}/1.0/{issue_key}

how to access issue through such REST API?

I've tried following without success:
    from jira import JIRA
    
    access_jira = JIRA("https://{my_jira_server}", basic_auth=({user}, {password}), options={'rest_path':u'something_different_than_api', 'rest_api_version':u'1.0'})


unfortunately only one of the options is set and second parameter is not taken into account.
e.g. 404 is returned with following url called:

    jira.utils.JIRAError: JiraError HTTP 404
	    text: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?><status><status-code>404</status-code><message>null for uri: {my_jira_server}/rest/api/1.0/serverInfo</message></status>
	    url: https://{my_jira_server}/rest/api/1.0/serverInfo

or if I leave rest_api_version set to "2" then:
    jira.utils.JIRAError: JiraError HTTP 404
        <title>Oops, you've found a dead link. - JIRA</title>
        url: https:/{my_jira_server}/rest/{something_different_than_api}/2/{issue_key}


how to make this things to work?

Thanks in advance,
bvccaneer

issue_title: Why am I getting "Endpoint request timed out" at the end instead of returned JSON?
readable() from File Object from aws_encryption_sdk.stream always False
issue_body:
I'm trying to upload a file to an S3 bucket with boto3's `upload_fileobj`. I wanted to create an StreamEncryptor so that I could chain together the encryption and upload without much copying. But I find that the StreamEncryptor object won't take because it's not compatible. 

Compatibility means calling `readable()` on the object if it exists, and ensuring it returns `True`. But in this case, my stream object returns `False` on the call. This is unexpected, because I can call `read()` and get data -- which seems wrong because `False` means `read()` should raise an error.

I can't see where readable is implemented in this class, it seems deep in the base/meta class. But maybe it's not implemented at all and defaulting to this value. But I think it should either return a semantically correct value, or not have this function at all.

issue_title: About multiple independent connections to same host
issue_body:
Hi,

First of all, thanks for sharing the package which works great in a single-connection-at-a-time scenario. :)

I'm in a scenario where I need two (or more) NTLM authenticated independent connections to the same host. These are requests to a REST interface with different URLs, say http://host/url_A?q_A and http://host/url_B?q_B.

My testing shows that these somewhat parallel requests sometimes work and other times get 401s. Following some investigation and digging through the code (both requests-ntlm and requests) I learned that:
* NTLM authentication is done on a per-connection basis.
* The auth process is completed after three request/responses on the connection.
* The currently published code does not ensure the same connection is reused (but pull request #51 does).
* The underlying requests (urllib3 actually) pool manager only returns connections from the pool on a hostname basis and not on a full-url basis.

This results in connections A (say for http://host/url_A?q_A) and B (for http://host/url_B?q_B) being switched along the three request/responses and, of course, leading to a failure.

If this is correct, would you have any suggestion on how to address my challenge? (I tried to investigate the possibility of providing an alternate pool manager implementation to urllib3 and did not find it... is there any?)

If my understanding is wrong, would you please point out my mistakes?

Thanks in advance for your feedback.

issue_title: How to create new Configs & Variables ?
issue_body:
The service supports and the documentation suggests that Configs and Variables resources creates methods (should) exist. Reviewing the documentation, I'm unable to find (expected?) create methods for these and reviewing the sources, it's unclear how these could be constructed.

Is it me? Or, are these methods missing?

"Create / interact with ... configs"
https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/latest/runtimeconfig/config.html

"Create / interact with ... variables"
https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/latest/runtimeconfig/variable.html

### Confirm
```
gcloud beta runtime-config configs list \
--project=${PROJECT}
```
```
NAME      DESCRIPTION
exists    Description
```
```
gcloud beta runtime-config configs variables list \
--config-name=exists \
--project=${PROJECT}
```
```
NAME       UPDATE_TIME
exists     2017-08-29T15:03:26.359064036Z
```

### Getters

```python
from google.cloud import runtimeconfig
client = runtimeconfig.Client()

config = client.config("exists")
print (config.exists())
print (config.get_variable("exists").exists())
```
Results:
```
True
True
```
### Setters?
```python
config = client.config("create")
print (config.exists())
config.create()
print (config.exists())

```
Results:

```
    config.create()
AttributeError: 'Config' object has no attribute 'create'
```
```python
print (config.exists())
variable = config.variable("test")
variable.create()
```
Results:
```
    variable.create()
AttributeError: 'Variable' object has no attribute 'create'
```

from troposphere import waf

And this value return --> <troposphere.waf.FieldToMatch object at 0x10d5be210>

Why have I this error?

issue_title: pex uses the wrong 'project name' for finding scripts in wheel files
issue_body:
There seems to be a bug in finders.py (https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/master/pex/finders.py#L252) where when constructing the path to look for scripts inside the wheel files, it uses the method `pkg_resources.DistInfoDistribution.project_name`, which works probably for most things but NOT if the project has hyphens in it

i noticed that this issue already existed: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/issues/383 but i do not think it is the same thing

so when you create setup.py, you give a project a name, mine is `windows-update-csv-to-wiki`

as you say in #383 , the wheel spec says that you can't have hyphens in the name, so the hyphens are translated to underscores. so I generated a wheel using `pip3 setup.py bdist_wheel` and this is what i get:

```
$ unzip -l dist/windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
Archive:  dist/windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
  Length      Date    Time    Name
---------  ---------- -----   ----
     6175  02-08-2018 22:41   windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data/scripts/windows_update_csv_to_wiki.py
       10  02-08-2018 22:41   windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info/DESCRIPTION.rst
      455  02-08-2018 22:41   windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info/metadata.json
        1  02-08-2018 22:41   windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info/top_level.txt
        1  02-08-2018 21:25   windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info/zip-safe
       92  02-08-2018 22:41   windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info/WHEEL
      358  02-08-2018 22:41   windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info/METADATA
      842  02-08-2018 22:41   windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info/RECORD
---------                     -------
     7934                     8 files
```

these are the commands i ran: (i followed the comment here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/issues/314#issuecomment-259221522 )

```
$ pip3 wheel --no-cache-dir --wheel-dir=./wheels -r requirements.txt
$ python3 setup.py bdist_wheel
$ cp dist/windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl wheels/
$ pex -vvv -f wheels/ --no-index -o "windows_updates_csv_to_wiki.pex" -r pex-requirements.txt -c "windows_update_csv_to_wiki.py"
```

after the pex command, i get: 

```
pex.pex_builder.InvalidExecutableSpecification: Could not find script 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki.py' in any distribution arrow 0.10.0, wheel 0.30.0, attrs 17.3.0, six 1.11.0, windows-update-csv-to-wiki 1.0.0, python-dateutil 2.6.1 within PEX!
```

I added some debug statements to `finders.py` to see what was going on, and i noticed:

```
dist:  windows-update-csv-to-wiki 1.0.0
-- wheel_scripts_dir:  bin
-- resource_listdir() result:  []
-- dist._provider._index() result {'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data/scripts': ['windows_update_csv_to_wiki.py'], 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data': ['scripts'], '': ['windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data', 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info'], 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info': ['DESCRIPTION.rst', 'metadata.json', 'top_level.txt', 'zip-safe', 'WHEEL', 'METADATA', 'RECORD']}
-- wheel_scripts_dir:  scripts
-- resource_listdir() result:  []
-- dist._provider._index() result {'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data/scripts': ['windows_update_csv_to_wiki.py'], 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data': ['scripts'], '': ['windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data', 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info'], 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info': ['DESCRIPTION.rst', 'metadata.json', 'top_level.txt', 'zip-safe', 'WHEEL', 'METADATA', 'RECORD']}
-- wheel_scripts_dir:  windows-update-csv-to-wiki-1.0.0.data/bin
-- resource_listdir() result:  []
-- dist._provider._index() result {'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data/scripts': ['windows_update_csv_to_wiki.py'], 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data': ['scripts'], '': ['windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data', 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info'], 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info': ['DESCRIPTION.rst', 'metadata.json', 'top_level.txt', 'zip-safe', 'WHEEL', 'METADATA', 'RECORD']}
-- wheel_scripts_dir:  windows-update-csv-to-wiki-1.0.0.data/scripts
-- resource_listdir() result:  []
-- dist._provider._index() result {'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data/scripts': ['windows_update_csv_to_wiki.py'], 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data': ['scripts'], '': ['windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.data', 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info'], 'windows_update_csv_to_wiki-1.0.0.dist-info': ['DESCRIPTION.rst', 'metadata.json', 'top_level.txt', 'zip-safe', 'WHEEL', 'METADATA', 'RECORD']}
```

notice how `wheel_scripts_dir`  (the path that it passes to the if statement containing `name in dist.resource_listdir(wheel_scripts_dir)` has hyphens, however all the file / folder entries inside the wheel have underscores, so of course it won't find it.

I looked around in the `pkg_resources.DistInfoDistribution` a bit and didn't see any magical method that will translate the project name into a 'wheel safe' name. I did however test that changing the line https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/master/pex/finders.py#L252

from 

```
  datadir_name = "%s-%s.data" % (dist.project_name, dist.version)
````

to 

```
  datadir_name = "%s-%s.data" % (dist.project_name.replace("-", "_"), dist.version)
````

works, but this is probably not the the most elegant solution

issue_title: Eliminate Jenkins Slave public IPs
issue_body: Once the Jenkins master is hosted in the same VPC as the slaves (2 different AWS accounts right now), it would make sense to leave only the Jenkins master public and use it as a bastion for ssh access to the slaves when slave inspection was needed.  Right now slaves launch with a public IP which is more surface area than we need, even if they are ephemeral.

issue_title: Implement deferred sources using ivy in the new engine
issue_body:
Deferred sources are a particularly isolated case of ivy usage, and should make a good first test case. Their current implementation is also quite awkward (multiple tasks and mutability in Target to inject sources later).

In the new engine, this should be accomplished via a task that can provide sources for a target given the address of an `unpacked_jars` target.

This is blocked-by-but-related-to #2978.

issue_body: We should blacklist `${HOME}/.cache/pants/lmdb_store`

issue_body: This is confusing territory that should be documented in code, by tests and with better Target and option docs.

issue_title: --cache-dir option in Ivy scope appears to be broken when used for resolve.
issue_body:
As per discussion in Peiyu's comment https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/3632/

Passing the `--ivy-cache-dir` in OSS pants triggers a trace. Seemingly no matter what value is passed(under the buildroot, workdir, otherwise):

     FAILURE: Classpath entry 
     /Users/mateo/.ivy2/pants/com.squareup.testing.protolib/protolib-test/jars/protolib-test-1.0.1.jar 
     for target 3rdparty:protobuf-test-import is located outside the working directory
     "/Users/mateo/dev/pants/.pants.d". 

My guess is that the resolves need to be invalidated when passing a new cachedir - ie that the cachedir should be in the resolve fingerprint or whatever equivalent mechanism it uses. A quick look suggests to me that the jars are being reported as previously fetched even when this flag is set - even when the flag is set to a bogus directory. BUt we don't use Ivy for resolution so this pipeline is pretty unknown to me.

To repro is simple:

```
mateo:pants mateo$ ./pants compile --ivy-cache-dir=/home/mateo/not/pants examples/src/java::

00:02:57 00:00 [main]
               (To run a reporting server: ./pants server)
00:02:57 00:00   [setup]
00:02:57 00:00     [parse]
               Executing tasks in goals: bootstrap -> imports -> unpack-jars -> deferred-sources -> gen -> jvm-platform-validate -> resolve -> compile
00:02:57 00:00   [bootstrap]
00:02:57 00:00     [jar-dependency-management]
00:02:57 00:00     [bootstrap-jvm-tools]
00:02:57 00:00   [imports]
00:02:57 00:00     [ivy-imports]
FAILURE: Classpath entry /Users/mateo/.ivy2/pants/com.squareup.testing.protolib/protolib-test/jars/protolib-test-1.0.1.jar \
for target 3rdparty:protobuf-test-import is located outside the working directory\
"/Users/mateo/dev/pants/.pants.d".


00:02:57 00:00   [complete]
               FAILURE
```

issue_title: Add `apilib` module
issue_body:
Add an `apilib` module providing basic boilerplate functionality for implementing API objects for data repositories. In particular, there should be
- commonly applicable validators for data objects based on the `attr` package
- a base class for API objects, taking a path to a directory as single argument.

issue_title: lr_scheduler seems to be reset if training is resumed
issue_body:
Hi,

if training is resumed the learning rate scheduler seems to be reset.

This seems to be the case because the class OptimizerConfig contains the learning rate scheduler both directly in lr_scheduler and also in the dict params, and the module.init_optimizer function relies on the dict OptimizerConfig.params but not on OptimizerConfig.lr_scheduler.

But here:
https://github.com/awslabs/sockeye/blob/da518f1c6e90e08e2a1f8d2888608121f75b3d67/sockeye/training.py#L989-L993
only the lr_scheduler is updated but not the params dict.

A quick fix might be adding

`self.optimizer_config.params['lr_scheduler'] = self.optimizer_config.lr_scheduler`

before

https://github.com/awslabs/sockeye/blob/da518f1c6e90e08e2a1f8d2888608121f75b3d67/sockeye/training.py#L993

But overall it looks dangerous to have the learning rate scheduler both in lr_scheduler and in the dict params within the class OptimizerConfig.

issue_title: Use existing API Gateway API when adding Lambda Api Trigger
issue_body:
Is it possible to add an `ApiTrigger` event or resource (to a Lambda Function) that adds a new endpoint to an existing API Gateway API?

Rather than having to create a new API whenever I deploy a single function, I would like to be able to create a new resource in an existing API that serves as a lambda trigger.

issue_title: AutoPublishAlias: Support updating version when things other than CodeUri Changes
issue_body:
**Description:**

Per the documentation, AutoPublishAlias only supports publishing another function version when the CodeUri changes. While having AutoPublishAlias in its current state is still beneficial, I have found this has caused much confusion and even bugs that took quite some time to track down. Specifically, when things such as environment variables are changed on the stack.

The "gotcha" that occurs is because an environment variable might be passed as a stack parameter (for instance). When doing a stack update and updating the parameter, everything appears to execute cleanly. The stack change set picks up that the function needs to be updated, etc and the update is marked as successful. This causes the false belief that your update got applied successfully, only on close inspection of the lambda function do you discover that the lambda function was updated, but a new version was not tied to the alias.

I am curious if there is plans to remove this limitation? While the documentation does spell this out clearly, unless someone updating the stack knows that function(s) in the stack are using the `AutoPublishAlias` feature and knows about this caveat - they they might think an update was successful with no other action needed.

issue_title: Use multipart upload to avoid base backup socket errors
issue_body:
I'm seeing errors uploading backups similar to https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/wal-e/KwbXdasIDUE when uploading from a local VM with slow networking.

Perhaps these problems could be managed better by using multipart uploading when the file size is over a threshold, so only failed parts need retrying. See: http://boto.readthedocs.org/en/latest/s3_tut.html#storing-large-data

The best upload/download performance I've seen is with the aws-cli utility which puts files to s3 using multiple parts in parallel using a threaded executor: https://github.com/aws/aws-cli/blob/master/awscli/customizations/s3/s3handler.py

migrated from trello tasks:
* https://trello.com/c/hBQu1UzZ/567-import-export-missing-content-is-not-gracefully-handled
* https://trello.com/c/3YGyf0v0/569-learn-app-missing-content-is-not-gracefully-handled

When content is referenced by a DB but missing the content folder, this is handled poorly in during import/export, when viewing in the Learn app, and potentially other situations. We should ensure:

* the server has proper checks and error handling to deal with content referenced by the DB but not available on disk
* the client has proper checks and error handling to show the user an indication that content is missing

Shown below: an example of errors while importing a set of channels where 3 large videos were deleted from the content:

issue_title: Allow passing memoryview/buffers to send
issue_body:
As nanomsg doesn't provide multipart messages it's convenient to create a BytesIO and dump a sequence of pickles into it. That way the "parts" of the message can be recovered at the other side by means of an equivalent sequence of pickle loads.

But the current implementation of Socket.send in nnpy requires the memoryview associated with the BytesIO to be transformed to bytes first:

```
TypeError: initializer for ctype 'char[19]' must be a bytes or list or tuple, not memoryview
```

This is not efficient, as it implies an extra copy of the message. But it seems the cffi guys implemented a conversion from buffer to char array:

https://bitbucket.org/cffi/cffi/issues/65/support-memoryview-to-char-conversion
https://bitbucket.org/cffi/cffi/issues/47/creating-a-cdata-from-a-buffer

I think it's important to support this in nnpy, as nanomsg is intended to be fast and require as little copy as possible.

issue_title: Implement workaround for NI-SCOPE simulation bug
issue_body:
[This thing I ran into on my test machine](https://github.com/ni/nimi-python/pull/570#issuecomment-342031324) and renders [nimi-bot](https://github.com/ni/nimi-python/wiki/nimi‐bot-System-Test-machine) inoperable every now and then...

Turns out it's a known bug in NI-SCOPE (internal bug 657567 and/or 565504).

The workaround is to simulate a device that's not based on the DAQmx framework (5122) and instead simulate one of the newer devices. This will also make things faster!

issue_title: Use model(**inputs) rather than model.forward(**inputs)
issue_body:
Follow up from email discussion, we should change usages of `model.forward(**inputs)` to `model(**inputs)` as the invoked `__call__` method for `torch.nn.Module` also includes some handling of hooks (code [here](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/53b01527f446c1940e2dc1c2023575e51d93919c/torch/nn/modules/module.py#L316)). 

I don't think there are currently known issues from this, so low urgency, but could arise, say, if adding hooks to a model and then running predictions.

My initial experiment is just to see if BiDAF with no modification can handle Squad 2.0 with a fixed answer for unanswerable questions.

Here's my code for modifying Squad 2.0:

```
from copy import deepcopy
import json

break_idx = 46

def convert_squad(data):
    sample_train = { "version":"2.0", "data":list() }

    for i,item in enumerate(data["data"]):
        
        if i == break_idx:
            break     
        
        ic = deepcopy(item)

        for j,p in enumerate(ic["paragraphs"]):

            # 1. Update context
            answer_start = 0
        
            # 2. Loop over qa dicts and reduct to fit squad 1.1 spec
            for q in p["qas"]:
                if not q["is_impossible"]:
                    q.pop('is_impossible')
                else:
                    q.pop('plausible_answers')
                    q.pop('is_impossible')
                    q["answers"].append( { 'text':  p["context"][answer_start],
                                           'answer_start': answer_start } )
        sample_train["data"].append(ic)
    return sample_train

train = json.loads(open("train-v.2.0.json", "r").read())
dev   = json.loads(open("dev-v.2.0.json", "r").read())

train_cv = convert_squad(train)
dev_cv = convert_squad(dev)

with open('squad_fc2/full_train.json', 'w') as outfile:
    json.dump(train_cv, outfile)
    
with open('squad_fc2/full_dev.json', 'w') as outfile:
    json.dump(dev_cv, outfile)
```

issue_title: Allow for configuring a JSON serializer
issue_body:
Right now I’m trying to import 11 GB of data and the ETA is 20h. :)  I’ve noticed, that you use the standard library’s JSON encoder which is the slowest of them all (except on PyPy).

issue_title: music21.midi.realtime.play does not play ties correctly on OS X Sierra
issue_body:
First things first: This project is absolutely incredible. A couple of other developers and I have been building an application for the past couple months using music21 as the primary backend technology, and I cannot thank all of you enough for the enormous amount of work you all have put into this massive project. 

That said, I came across an issue recently that is a bit problematic for our particular use case. We've been using music21.midi.realtime.play in order to play back pieces that our users compose, and we ran into a strange problem involving playback of notes connected with ties. This simple python program is enough to replicate the issue: 
```
import music21

# Create the stream
s = music21.stream.Stream()
# Make two notes with a tie between them
note1 = music21.note.Note("C#4", 1.0)
note1.tie = music21.tie.Tie("start")
note2 = music21.note.Note("C#4", 2.0)
note2.tie = music21.tie.Tie("stop")
# Insert the notes in the stream
s.insert(0.0, note1)
s.insert(1.0, note2)
# Attempt to playback the stream
music21.midi.realtime.StreamPlayer(s).play() # 2 notes play instead of 1
# Check the score in a GUI to make sure the tie is present
s.show()
```

Correct me if I'm wrong, but I'm pretty sure the whole point of ties is to ensure a note keeps sounding beyond what a single note head can notate. Fortunately, it appears that Musescore and other GUI applications display the tie correctly, which means that the tie is indeed interpretable. However, this does not appear to be the case on playback - where only 1 continuous note should sound, 2 are played. 

I've only poked a little bit into the music21 source code - there's quite a lot of it - but this seems like something that should be doable with a little tooling around. One potential workaround could be storing semantic note duration (as it appears on the page) and playback note duration (as one would hear the piece when played) as separate values. I imagine this could get a little hairy when trying to create ties across separate Stream objects, though. I don't know how feasible it is in all cases, but it may be something to consider implementing. 

It also may very well be that this issue is local to my machine environment, and if so it's definitely a low priority bug that we can probably hack around. But if it's not unique to my architecture, it seems like something that could probably be investigated and improved upon. 

Thanks again for all your hard work! :)

issue_title: Azure: Switch provision scripts over to using ARM template
issue_body:
Currently, the Azure provision script uses v1 of the Azure API, which from what I understand is being deprecated in favor of [Azure Resource Manager](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/documentation/articles/resource-group-overview/). We should pull in the ARM [template](https://github.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates/pull/473) that's already been written for Deis 1.9, polish it, and use it in the project in place of the existing Python script.

I'd also like to use Terraform instead of the Azure CLI to submit the template, but it appears that Terraform doesn't yet support any ARM resources for Azure: https://terraform.io/docs/providers/azure/index.html

Using the ARM template will also address concerns such as #4332 (using an availability set for Deis) and #3323.

/cc @sgoings

issue_title: Ensure that GlobusHTTPResponse objects are serializable/deserializable
issue_body:
Previously, it was possible to pass response objects to pickle/unpickle safely.
However, now that we have a backref from responses to the clients which generated them, we're in a bit of a... bind.

Clients have their own loggers (well, LoggerAdapters, but whatever), which are not safe to pickle.

Pickle docs state that you can define custom instance methods to use instead of direct `__dict__` inspection.
getstate: https://docs.python.org/2/library/pickle.html#object.__getstate__
and
setstate: https://docs.python.org/2/library/pickle.html#object.__setstate__

If I'm reading these right, it might be sufficient to define getstate and setstate in the base client class as follows:
```python
def __getstate__(self):
    d = dict(self.__dict__)  # copy
    del d['logger']
    return d

def __setstate__(self, d):
    self.__dict__.update(d)
    self.logger = self._genlogger()  # replace the step in __init__ with a helper
```

In addition to these additions, tests for serializing/deserializing responses with un/pickle would be needed.
Ideally, we would test on token introspect responses, which are the most likely response object to be cached (and therefore pickled).

issue_title: features using wrong datatype: scalar string instead of list?
issue_body:
I have not had the chance to see if this is an actual bug, but it looks very suspicious so I wanted to report it.

Features are represented as a list in meta.yaml:
```
build:
    features:
        - intel
        - vc9
```

..but they end up in the index.json metadata file as a scalar string. Dependencies are retained as a list in the json:

```
"depends": [
    "intelpython",
    "vc"
  ],
  "features": "intel vc9",
```

I haven't dug into the code that uses "features" to see if this is a real problem, but if they are to be treated as a list, I would think they would simply be kept as a list.

issue_title: cache_fn_url() changed in 4.3.x
issue_body:
In conda < 4.3 the function was simply:
```
def cache_fn_url(url):
    md5 = hashlib.md5(url.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
    return '%s.json' % (md5[:8],)
```
however, in 4.3, an additional `url = url.rstrip('/')` has been added.  Therefore the resulting filename is different.

This means that updating to conda 4.3.2 will automatically invalidate all existing caches of `repodata` from any channel.  I'm not sure if this is going to be a big problem, but I assume that people who operate in offline mode are going to suddenly not be able to do operations which involve rely on having a local cache of repodata.  I'm thinking about air-gapped environments.  The easiest solution would be to restore the old functionality.

issue_title: --use-local breaks on missing file:/// repos
issue_body:
I've got a system install in /opt (root owned)  I run conda-build (as a local user, not as root) and so the package is written to /Users/hugo/conda-bld/osx-64/.    Then if I try to install the package using conda install <pgname> --use-local, I get an error because file:///opt/anaconda/conda-bld/osx-64 is not a valid url (presumably because I've never built a package there, and so there is no repo there)

If I run conda-build with sudo, then the package is built into /opt/anaconda/conda-bld, and then the problem goes away

My guess is that the fix is that --use-local should be robust to missing local repos?

issue_title: No module named conda.cli.main
issue_body:
Hi all, I encountered problem with conda after I updated packages
After executing `conda update --all`, I freed up some spaces with `conda clean -pity`
It is similar to issue #2200 
```bash
username@userpc:~> conda
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/username/anaconda/bin/conda", line 4, in <module>
    from conda.cli.main import main
ImportError: No module named conda.cli.main
username@userpc:~> conda list
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/username/anaconda/bin/conda", line 4, in <module>
    from conda.cli.main import main
ImportError: No module named conda.cli.main
username@userpc:~> . activate myenv
bash: activate: No such file or directory
username@userpc:~> which activate
which: no activate in ($PATH)
username@userpc:~> 
```
I checked the root environment directory and found that some symbolic link files are broken, eg. activate, deactivate
Those files are pointed to conda .pkgs directory, which was cleaned with `conda clean -pity`
Any ideas how to recover it, or reinstall `conda` ?

issue_title: conda 4.3.6 fails to respect specified versions in an update all
issue_body:
With conda 4.2.15 you can specify the versions for specific packages in an `update --all`
```
C:\dev\code\sandbox> conda list ^conda$
# packages in environment at C:\bin\Anaconda:
#
conda                     4.2.15                   py35_0
C:\dev\code\sandbox> conda update icu=57.1 qt=5.6 conda=4.2.15 --all
Fetching package metadata ...............
Solving package specifications: ..........

With conda 4.3.6 the version specifications are ignored:

issue_title: Bad package that breaks /bin will trip up conda
issue_body:
A build script like this will break conda, when it tries to link "conda" into the bin directory.  This script accidentally copies the file into a file named bin and not into a directory bin.  This builds successfully, but breaks conda when installing.
```
gcc -O -o pstree pstree.c
cp pstree $PREFIX/bin
```

We should test for this since it is a common and easy error to make.

issue_title: Build:  should allow overriding meta.yaml file
issue_body:
It should be possible to override values in the meta.yaml file from the command-line.  A very practical example is overriding the build number so it works nicely with binstar.  Building on Jenkins, I can supply a build number at build time.  Currently, I use sed to modify the meta.yaml file's build number, but it would be much nicer if i could override the build number directly:

```
conda build <recipe_dir> --build_number $build
```

I don't know the best way to extend this to all values in the meta.yaml file, but there are a few parameters that would hit almost all of the use cases:
  - version
  - url, fn, git_url, md5
  - git_tag
  - number

issue_title: 'allocation' returns failure when already optimized
issue_body:
I have a cron job that runs {delete, allocation, optimize} nightly and house it with a script that intercepts STDOUT and curator's return code in order to determine success of the run. I have some expectation of idempotence in these operations and so I use the same selection parameters each time.

In the case where an operation would be applied twice to the same criteria (e.g. right after), I notice that 'delete' will WARN (+msgout?) and return zero ("No indices matched provided args"), where 'allocation' will WARN and return one:

```
Running command: "/usr/local/mycorp/prod/local/python_2.6/bin/curator --host 'elasticsearch' --loglevel 'WARNING' --port '9200' --timeout '21600' allocation --rule 'tier=warm' indices --newer-than '30' --older-than '1' --time-unit 'days' --timestring '%Y.%m.%d'"
WARNING   Very large list of indices.  Breaking it up into smaller chunks.
WARNING   No indices to act on.
WARNING   No indices to act on.
WARNING   No indices to act on.
WARNING   Job did not complete successfully.
```

I understand that I should be using the API for my case and ought not to expect the CLI verbs to map to API functions directly, but should these be unified somehow? Is there a decision on what constitutes success?

issue_title: RequestsHttpConnection::perform_request except clause uses possibly-unbound 'response' variable
issue_body:
When using helpers.bulk (which is fantastic!), I received:
    UnboundLocalError: local variable 'response' referenced before assignment

This was raised by connection/http_requests.py RequestsHttpConnection::perform_request(); see: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/master/elasticsearch/connection/http_requests.py#L70

The (small) error is that the `try:` block in that function attempts to set `response`, but this assignment could fail if an exception is thrown from requests.Session::request(); in this case, `response` is unbound in the `except:` clause, but is unconditionally dereferenced. 

An example of one possible patch; not fully tested:
https://github.com/brianfaull/elasticsearch-py/commit/89f51899a671ebd195eff22f20f624fa8783139f

Overview: Split the assembly of the request from the sending of the request, and use the request object (not the response object) for logging. Clearly there are other possibilities also.

This is not an issue for the analog within http_urllib3.py (however it seems that url and full_url/path_url arguments to self.log_request_fail() are transposed between http_urllib3.py and http_requests.py unless I misunderstand.)

I note your preference for signing the CLA and submitting a PR; please let me know if that is still your preference for a fix as small as this.

Many thanks for a wonderful and useful library!

issue_title: Parallel upload/download opening too many TCP/IP connections
issue_body:
The current implementation does not submit web requests through a session, which results in a new session being created and destroyed each time a request is sent. This results in a lot of time being spent in getaddrinfo calls when that information can be re-used. The solution to this is to execute requests through a session like in the below example:
```
self.global_session = requests.Session()
self.global_session.put(<url>, params=params, headers=headers, data=data, stream=stream)
```

note, though, that Session is not guaranteed to be thread safe, so session awareness will need to be passed down through each thread, resulting in one session per thread.

issue_title: Connector timeout includes time waiting for connector from pool
issue_body:
## Long story short

Typically connector timeouts means time waiting for a connection to a server, and does not include any time waiting for a connection from the pool

This is because the timeout is started here: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/aiohttp/client.py#L289

and in connect it will wait for a connector: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/aiohttp/connector.py#L382

## Expected behaviour

conn_timeout should only include active time waiting to connect to a server as this isn't an error and expected behavior.  I'd suggest moving the timeout to the connector. 

## Actual behaviour

If you make a large number of requests, and have limited number of connectors, your requests may timeout w/o even trying to connect to the server because no connectors are available.

## Steps to reproduce

create a large number of requests with a limited number of connectors with short conn_timeout.

## Your environment
Any platform with aiohttp 2.x

We can probably have a file just for constants and basic types like this and then reference them when needed. That way, we wouldn't have circular dependency.

issue_title: MatrixClient stores HS when logging in / registering, but not when creating with token
issue_body:
When a client instance logs in / registers, the response contains, among other things, a `home_server` field. Currently, this information is stored as a `.hs` member of the instance. However, if the MatrixClient is created with an exiting token, no such registration/login takes plave, hence no response is received, and the instance doesn't set this member -- which means that code can't rely on its being there.

At the moment, this information isn't used anywhere in the code, so probably the best thing to do is to just remove it.

issue_title: Background/Calibration handeling
issue_body:
Two issues which might need to be in MDS but I am not certain.
 1. How to associate calibration data with actual data
 1. How to associate background data with actual data

Both these are issues because they might be performed at any point during your total beamtime but need to be associated with all your data. Also the calibration/background might be different for different samples/runs. Finally, calibration data is most useful in the form of (at least for XPD) a dictionary of distances and detector angles, it would be very tedious (without an auto calibrator) to need to calibrate every time you touch your data. Thus it would be great to store this processed data with the calibration data somehow.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Add flag to run non-paired reads
GATK wants karyotypic order for HG19
issue_body:
Well you know, your reference files might be in karyotypic order and it'd be a damn shame if your reads weren't in karyotypic order because that would be a completely unrecoverable error now wouldn't it be. I mean, since all of our inputs are indexed, there's no possible way that we could use the index to read  the BAM file in random order.

Also, since we validate the order of ref sequences listed in all inputs, there's no possible way that we could see our reference, known-sites VCFs, and reads have the same ref sequence order, and skip the karyotypic order.

Anyways, word on the street is that you can disable this with `--unsafe`.

issue_title: BrowsableProductManager was never deprecated, complicating overrides to it and ProductManager
issue_body:
I'm failing to understand why `BrowsableProductManager` was never deprecated and/or removed. The docstrings suggest it could have been deprecated as early as Oscar 0.7. I realize that the vanilla Product model uses it, but why does it use `BrowsableProductManager` instead of `ProductManager().browsable()`? The way this currently works makes overriding the behavior unnecessarily complicated, since `BrowsableProductManager` doesn't inherit dynamically, so overriding BPM requires re-declaring it after overriding PM. Obviously, I could just set my Product's `browsable` manager to `ProductManager().browsable()`, but it seems like this should have been dealt with already.

This seems like something that has just been forgotten, since the docstrings don't appear to have been updated since at least 0.7. I will happily do the legwork, but if this setup is intentional, I'll leave it alone.

### Technical details

* Python version: 3.6
* Django version: 1.11
* Oscar version: 1.6

issue_title: Allow "Add Reference" to .NET Core class library that uses .NET Framework from a traditional class library
New tooling bits, next preview release?
issue_body:
I have a critical question about the timeline of the next tooling bits, as the guys at dotnet-docker are stuck on a CLI version that's completely broken for linux 4.6+ kernels.

Related:
https://github.com/dotnet/core/issues/171
https://github.com/dotnet/dotnet-docker/issues/61
https://github.com/dotnet/cli/issues/3681
and many more (ubuntu 16.04 issues etcetera)

As more and more distro's move to 4.6+ kernels and a lot of CI's  using these in turn have as well (including the CI we use) there are more and more people grinding to a halt with this CLI version inside docker containers.
On top of that there is no obvious fix but to compile the CLI from source and then include this in your own docker build. Which is really quite an embarrassing answer for a company like Microsoft to point to as the only fix for now. 

It seems this would require just a tiny bit of coordinated efforts to get resolved and I would love to know an estimate timeframe of the next tooling release with this fix.

EDIT: This is of course not exclusive to docker containers but to all dotnet cli instances running on 4.6+ kernels, I have the luck that my dev env is OSX and not an up-to-date linux distro.

issue_title: automatic upgrading downgrades to incompatible version
issue_body:
Tested in latest master, https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/commit/1a77c7385768e73851b2e4e685073fa39d4a2298

**Steps:**

1. git clone https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.git
2. cd letsencrypt
3. ./letsencrypt-auto

`letsencrypt: error: unrecognized arguments: --le-auto-phase2`

Cause: **letsencrypt-auto-source/letsencrypt-auto**
```python
REMOTE_VERSION=`"$LE_PYTHON" "$TEMP_DIR/fetch.py" --latest-version`
if [ "$LE_AUTO_VERSION" != "$REMOTE_VERSION" ]; then
...
fi
"$0" --le-auto-phase2 "$@"
```
will fetch the version from https://pypi.python.org/pypi/letsencrypt/json. Right now that version is "0.4.0". Since the check above only checks if the versions are different, the auto script will "upgrade" version "0.5.0.dev0" to "0.4.0", which does not know about the new argument, and attempt to run with that.

The changes were introduced in https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/commit/e8f6d4ff39e0d7cd4331a99a8941fc3771c5f07c

issue_title: CKEditorWidget is not compatible with SplitArrayField
issue_body:
When we try to use CKEditorWidget with SplitArrayField only TextArea is rendered. For Example: For form field: `options = SplitArrayField(forms.CharField(widget = CKEditorWidget()),size=4)` This will result in rendering TextArea boxes with no other attributes. **Solution Used :** I have traced back the issue and currently created a custom widget extending CKEditorWidget and finding required fields ``` class SplitArrayCKEditorWidget(CKEditorWidget): #Custom Widget which overrides get_context as #CKEDITOR Widget does not by SplitArray utilizes it def __init__(self,*args,**kwargs): super(SplitArrayCKEditorWidget,self).__init__(*args,**kwargs) self.template_name = 'ckeditor/split_array_widget.html' def get_context(self, name, value, attrs): json_encode = LazyEncoder().encode context = {} if value is None: value = '' final_attrs = self.build_attrs(self.attrs, attrs, name=name) self._set_config() external_plugin_resources = [[force_text(a), force_text(b), force_text(c)] for a, b, c in self.external_plugin_resources] context['widget'] = { 'final_attrs': flatatt(final_attrs), 'value': conditional_escape(force_text(value)), 'id': final_attrs['id'], 'config': json_encode(self.config), 'template_name':self.template_name, 'external_plugin_resources': json_encode(external_plugin_resources) } return context` ``` **Note** that this also needs custom template as following ``` {% load static %}
{{ widget.value }}
``` Basically with _widget_ ahead all the attributes of the source template. I understand this is raw hack, but might help others and can be corrected in future release.

from django.contrib.staticfiles.templatetags.staticfiles import static

```

issue_title: Default values for webargs
issue_body:
Now that webargs is using marshmallow internally, we specify default argument values using the `missing` parameter:
```
arg = fields.Str(missing='foo')
```

But apispec doesn't check the `missing` key when introspective parameter defaults--it only checks `default`. To get the correct swagger default, we have to specify both parameters:
```
arg = fields.Str(missing='foo', default='foo')
```

Which isn't ideal.

Since we're using the same logic to introspect fields and schemas that are used for serialization and deserialization, one approach would be to tell `field2property` and related methods whether they're introspecting load or dump. For the specific case of parameter defaults, we'd check `default` for dump and `missing` for load.

issue_title: Is this a typo of fields.ValidatedField._validated, line#1138
Provide positional information in errors dictionary when serializing a collection (many=True)
issue_body:
When validating a collection with invalid elements, the returned errors dictionary does not provide any information about the positions of invalid elements

Example:

```python
def validate_age(age):
    if age < 21:
        raise ValidationError('Must be over 21')

class UserSchema(Schema):
    age = fields.Number(validate=validate_age)

in_data = [{'age': 24}, {'age': 19}]
data, errors = UserSchema(many=True).load(in_data)
```

### Current

```python
errors  # => {'age': ['Must be over 21']}
```

### Possible solution

Provide positional information in a nested dictionary, keyed by index.

```python
errors  # => {'age': {1: ['Must be over 21']}}
```

issue_title: Support object polymorphism
issue_body:
In my application, I have an API endpoint like so:

`/api/activities/`

In my database, I have several different types of Activity models, all inheriting from a common class. I made a schema like so:

```
class ActivitySchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = models.Activity
```

And then have flask-restful handle the api endpoint itself, querying the database and passing the objects to marshmallow-alchemy.

However, marshmallow-sqlalchemy serializes the objects as if they were all of the base Activity type. It would be nice if there was some support like this:

```
class ActivitySchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = models.Activity
        generate_polymorphic_schemas = True
```

Which would use sqlalchemy's inspection to view all polymorpic subclasses of `Meta.model`, store them on `ActivitySchema`, and then when serializing/deserializing use the appropriate class.

Thoughts?

If this is a desirable feature, I can try to make a PR implementing such functionality.

issue_title: Excessive queries on load (nested schemas)
issue_body:
We had this issue for a long time, but now it's starting to become really bad for us. See the example below:

```
from flask import Flask
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy, get_debug_queries
from marshmallow import fields
from marshmallow_sqlalchemy import ModelSchema
from datetime import datetime

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:////tmp/test.db'
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_ECHO'] = False
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS'] = True
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_RECORD_QUERIES'] = True
db = SQLAlchemy(app)


class User(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True)
    email = db.Column(db.String(120), unique=True)
    created_datetime = db.Column(db.DateTime(), default=datetime.utcnow)
    updated_datetime = db.Column(db.DateTime(), default=datetime.utcnow,
                                 onupdate=datetime.utcnow)

    def __repr__(self):
        return '<User %r>' % self.username


class URL(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey(User.id))
    url = db.Column(db.String())

    user = db.relationship(User, backref=db.backref('urls'))

    def __repr__(self):
        return '<URL %r>' % self.url


def create_user():
    user1 = User(username='user1', email="user1@example.com")
    user1.urls.append(URL(url="http://a"))
    user1.urls.append(URL(url="http://b"))
    user2 = User(username='user2', email="user2@example.com")
    user2.urls.append(URL(url="http://c"))
    user2.urls.append(URL(url="http://d"))

    db.session.add(user1)
    db.session.add(user2)
    db.session.commit()


class UserSchema(ModelSchema):
    urls = fields.Nested('URLSchema', many=True)

    class Meta:
        model = User
        sqla_session = db.session


class URLSchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = URL
        sqla_session = db.session


user_schema = UserSchema()


@app.route("/")
def hello():
    user = User.query.options(db.joinedload('urls')).filter_by(id=1).first()
    user_dump = user_schema.dump(user)
    user_schema.load(user_dump.data, instance=user)
    for query in get_debug_queries():
        print("%s %r\n------" % (query.statement, query.parameters))

    return ''


if __name__ == "__main__":
    db.drop_all()
    db.create_all()
    create_user()
    app.run(port=9999, debug=True)

```

Results in:

```
SELECT anon_1.user_id AS anon_1_user_id, anon_1.user_username AS anon_1_user_username, anon_1.user_email AS anon_1_user_email, anon_1.user_created_datetime AS anon_1_user_created_datetime, anon_1.user_updated_datetime AS anon_1_user_updated_datetime, "URL_1".id AS "URL_1_id", "URL_1".user_id AS "URL_1_user_id", "URL_1".url AS "URL_1_url"
FROM (SELECT user.id AS user_id, user.username AS user_username, user.email AS user_email, user.created_datetime AS user_created_datetime, user.updated_datetime AS user_updated_datetime
FROM user
WHERE user.id = ?
 LIMIT ? OFFSET ?) AS anon_1 LEFT OUTER JOIN "URL" AS "URL_1" ON anon_1.user_id = "URL_1".user_id (1, 1, 0)
------
SELECT user.id AS user_id, user.username AS user_username, user.email AS user_email, user.created_datetime AS user_created_datetime, user.updated_datetime AS user_updated_datetime
FROM user
WHERE user.id = ? (1,)
------
SELECT user.id AS user_id, user.username AS user_username, user.email AS user_email, user.created_datetime AS user_created_datetime, user.updated_datetime AS user_updated_datetime
FROM user
WHERE user.id = ? (1,)
------
SELECT "URL".id AS "URL_id", "URL".user_id AS "URL_user_id", "URL".url AS "URL_url"
FROM "URL"
WHERE "URL".id = ?
 LIMIT ? OFFSET ? (1, 1, 0)
------
SELECT "URL".id AS "URL_id", "URL".user_id AS "URL_user_id", "URL".url AS "URL_url"
FROM "URL"
WHERE "URL".id = ?
 LIMIT ? OFFSET ? (2, 1, 0)
------
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Mar/2017 10:32:47] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 200 -
```

It's a bit hard to parse but you might notice that the `dump` part  is 1 query with an `OUTER JOIN` which is exactly what I expected, however when loading with an attached instance it performs these additional queries.

I think I understand why this happens, you need this because you might need to do an update, but if the instance is given with all the associated objects, the queries above are not really need. 

In an ideal world I would expect: No queries to the DB when doing dump/load when `instance` is provided.

Is there a way to have no side effects when doing `load`? This is a constant source of big bugs for us.

issue_title: No OAuth 2 token endpoint option in ztoken
issue_body:
There is no option available for specifying a different token endpoint, only the authorization endpoint. 

```bash
rreis@tesseract:~/go/src/github.bus.zalan.do/rreis/dcskipper$ ztoken token --help
Usage: ztoken token [OPTIONS]

  Create a new Platform IAM token or use an existing one.

Options:
  -n, --name TOKEN_NAME           Custom token name (will be stored)
  -a, --authorize-url AUTH_URL    OAuth 2 Authorization Endpoint URL to
                                  generate access token
  -c, --client-id CLIENT_ID       Client ID to use
  -p, --business-partner-id PARTNER_ID
                                  Business Partner ID to use
  -r, --refresh                   Force refresh of the access token
  -h, --help                      Show this message and exit.
```

One usecase where this can be useful is in the Sandbox:

```bash
ztoken token -a https://sandbox.identity.zalando.com/oauth2/authorize -t https://sandbox.identity.zalando.com/oauth2/token
```

If only the authorize endpoint is overriden, ztoken still returns a token based on its current configuration, if it sill has a valid refresh token.

To avoid confusion, ztoken should behave:
- If `-a` is specified, ask **always** for the refresh token endpoint
- If `-t` is specified, ask **always** for the authorization endpoint
- When any of the previous options are used, **never** override the existing refresh token from the original configuration.

issue_title: Adding Huber Loss to objectives
issue_body:
The huber loss is the least squared error if the absolute difference is less than a threshold(which is defined to be 1 for SmoothL1Loss), else it is `δ (|d|−δ/2)` where d is the absolute error. This is vastly used in regression problems and is useful in building models that generates bounding boxes over different objects in an image. 
The draft of the above described is [here](https://gist.github.com/Sentient07/bade6167bc923a4f0bf4a14196065cf7#file-theano-fastrcnn-py-L20-L24). If it is good to have this is Lasagne, I will open a PR :)

issue_title: Configure timeouts
issue_body:
The user should be given the option to specify how long to wait for cell execution timeout. The current timeout is set to ~half an hour (2000 s).

One option is to piggy-back on the `timeout` option added by the [timeout plugin](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pytest-timeout). If not, we should ensure that we choose an option name that won't conflict, like `nbval-cell-timeout` (or maybe a shorter version).

issue_title: Error: Invariant Violation: Minified React error #254
issue_body:
Any Qobj output from the `qutip` package causes the following error:

> Error: Invariant Violation: Minified React error #254; visit https://reactjs.org/docs/error-decoder.html?invariant=254&args[]=node for the full message or use the non-minified dev environment for full errors and additional helpful warnings.

Tested with qutip version 4.3.1 and nteract 0.11.4

Full text of error (found via link above) is: 
>Element ref was specified as a string (node) but no owner was set. This could happen for one of the following reasons: 1. You may be adding a ref to a functional component 2. You may be adding a ref to a component that was not created inside a component's render method 3. You have multiple copies of React loaded See https://fb.me/react-refs-must-have-owner for more information.

The output from `qutip` has been problematic before (see #2509) not sure if this is related. If there is a better way to craft the output on the `qutip` end, I'm happy to work on a fix from that end. I don't yet know enough about nteract to submit a solution from this side of things.

issue_title: Pickle a Path?
issue_body:
[solve.py.txt](https://github.com/angr/angr/files/1056377/solve.py.txt)

Guys,

I posted to the angr mailing list a few weeks back about not being able to pickle (to file) a path object in angr.  I was able to find a test case where it can be reproduced (the file I submitted named solve.py.txt).  This file should be placed in the angr-doc-master/examples/fauxware directory and run against the fauxware example file.  It is a slight mod on the original solve.py file that came in the fauxware directory.  This will demonstrate the problem.

So the problem is not that pickling the path object fails, but rather it succeeds, and then when pickle loads the object back in, it is a drastically larger size, and the path.history object is not the same as the path.history object that was originally pickled.  

This could just be an issue with how you have the angr/path_history.py PathHistory object setup (e.g. path_history._parent)...or there's a slight chance it's a bug in python's pickle.py.   I ran my tests using python 2.7.13

Thanks so much for your help.
Ejm

 (u'SessionID', u'0'),
 (u'OpenID', u'o011111111')
])

I've updated simple_goto.py (see branch tmp_getting_started_dkpy2 which will be merged in #320).

If I re-run the script when the vehicle is airborne the system still reports ">>> ☺ARMING MOTORS" etc. Yes, the script does set ``vehicle.armed = True`` but it is already armed and flying and the vehicle will ignore this command. So ideally in this case the message should not be shown.

```
Arming motors
Taking off!
 Altitude:  20.0100002289
Reached target altitude
Going to first point...
>>> ☺ARMING MOTORS
>>> ☺Initialising APM...
```

Note, I appreciate that perhaps this should just be ignored. I'm just raising the issue so we make a decision and I can document the behaviour. Ditto with ``Initialising APM``. Other alternatives 

- change the message so that it is clear that we're sending a command only
- remove all auto-generated messages - let the script author define their own messages if needed.

issue_title: Load metadata without loading data
issue_body:
It would be beneficial to users of satpy and for code clarity if satpy reader's could load the metadata for a dataset without having to load the data.

1. If a user loads just the metadata first, how do they then ask for the data?
2. How does satpy use previously loaded metadata when data is loaded? Does it waste the time to recompute it? Cache? Where is it cached? Caching in the main level reader probably makes the most sense since it would have the fully combined/merged metadata and could then take the concatenated data arrays and create a new Dataset object.
3. What happens to areas? Are they loaded with the rest of the metadata? Is there another keyword to load only metadata but not with areas? What's the default?

Any other issues with trying to make this work?

issue_title: Optionally do not decode to UTF-8
issue_body: By default, `.decode()` returns UTF-8. Some systems, however, expect headers to be bytes and to worry about it themselves. We may optionally want to disable this automatic decoding of the text encoding, and instead to return byte strings.

issue_title: jobs.select is broken again
issue_body:
I expect the following: ```jobs.select(id=1322)``` to return the job with id 1322, however when I run this command I get my whole repo returned as a jobs slice.

However: ```jobs.select(1322, 1322)``` works as expected and returns the one job.

The jobs.select method is used __heavily__ within the monitoring system and should not be doing this.
(I suspect fixing this would also fix #286.)

This just totaled my entire repo of custom jobs which are not all loadable at runtime due to a combination of this and #164.

Can we fix this for 6.1.19 please.

issue_title: cross-link Kinto docs to Cliquet docs instead of copying
issue_body:
Use the Sphinx "intersphinx" plugin to link to Cliquet documentation in a disciplined way rather than just copying a random amount of documentation out of another project. Most of the Kinto documentation should be defined with reference to corresponding documentation in Cliquet -- for example, Kinto permissions should link to Cliquet permissions, and Kinto records should link to Cliquet resources.

[N.B. that of course this might depend on the outcome of https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/issues/687.]

issue_title: Session Handling
issue_body:
I have been trying to login to a website and then scrap some sub-urls only accesible after signing in.
    

    def start_requests(self):
        script = """
            function main(splash)
                splash:init_cookies(splash.args.cookies)
                assert(splash:go(splash.args.url))
                splash:set_viewport_full()

                local search_input = splash:select('input[name=username]')
                search_input:send_text("MY_USERNAME")

                splash:evaljs("document.getElementById('password').value = 'MY_PASSWORD';")

                local submit_button = splash:select('input[name=signin]')
                submit_button:click()

                local entries = splash:history()
                local last_response = entries[#entries].response

                return {
                    cookies = splash:get_cookies(),
                    headers = last_response.headers,
                    html = splash:html()
                }
              end
        """

        yield scrapy_splash.SplashRequest(
            url='https://www.website.com/login',
            callback=self.after_login,
            endpoint='execute',
            cache_args=['lua_source'],
            args={'lua_source': script}
        )

    def after_login(self, response):
        with open('after_login.html') as out:
            out.write(response.body.decode(''utf-8))

        script = """
            function main(splash)
                splash:init_cookies(splash.args.cookies)
                assert(splash:go(splash.args.url))
                splash:set_viewport_full()
                assert(splash:wait(10))

                return {
                    cookies = splash:get_cookies(),
                    html = splash:html()
                }
              end
        """
        yield scrapy_splash.SplashRequest(
            url='https://www.website.com/search?tools',
            callback=self.parse,
            endpoint='execute',
            cookies = response.data['cookies'],
            headers = response.data['headers'],
            args={'lua_source': script},
        )

    def parse(self, response):
        with open('search_result.html', 'w+') as out:
            out.write(response.body.decode('utf-8'))

I am following the instructions in [Session Handling](https://github.com/scrapy-plugins/scrapy-splash#session-handling). First, I login and I am begin redirected to the home page, this is correctly saved in **login.html** (Login is working). Then I take the cookies and set them in the second SplashRequest to search, however the response in **search_result.html** is that user is not logged in. What am I missing or doing wrong in order to persiste the session in different SplashRequests?

Regards,

issue_title: Pointcloud : Texture coordinates are always {0.0, 0.0}
Adapt is not parsing unknown entities like number
issue_body:
Take this example.

Home Automation Intent Parser.

Automation Phrases used will be like below:

```
"Mycroft Set First floor Office Temperature at Twenty"
"Mycroft Switch On Ground floor Kitchen Table Light"
"Mycroft Switch Off Second floor Bathroom Heater"
```

Automation Intent Parser:

```
action_keyword = ["Set", "On", "Off"] ---> Require
floor_keyword = ["Ground", "First", "Second"] ---> Require
room_keyword =["Kitchen","Office","Bathroom"] ---> Require
device_keyword =["Heater", "Cooler" "Ceiling", "Table"] ---> Require
```

Q:- How Intent Parser will handle "Temperature at Twenty" ?
Other two phrases (2 and 3 ) looks ok, as entities are known.
Q:- How Automation Skill will get this number Twenty in json before setting office temperature?

Twenty is not a known entity, Do we need to registered another keyword with intent parser ?
    temperature_keyword = ["one", "two"........................."fifty five"] ---> Require / Optional

If we make temperature_keyword optional and Automation Intent Parser detect no Temperature Value in phrase, then it will definitely pass null to Skill in json for temperature value.
Suppose Automation Skill put a check on Temperature value (which is null) and ask again for Temperature,
user replied "set it at Twenty",
In this case do we need another Temperature Intent Parser to parse Temperature Value?
..........................................................................................
Take another example :

A Voice Calculator

```
"Calculate Five Thousand Divide by Twenty Five"
```

Calculator Intent Parser
    calci_keyword=["Calculate", "Calci"] ------> Require
    operation_keyword=["Multiply","Divide","Plus","Minus"] ---> Require

What about "Five Thousand" and " Twenty Five" ?
How Intent Parser will deal with Numbers ?
..........................................................................................

Stock Exchange Intent Parser

```
"BUY Thousand Share of APPLE Limit two hundred point five"
"SELL Twenty Lot of ORACLE Market"
```

What about "Thousand" and " two hundred point five" ?
How Intent Parser will deal with Numbers ?

issue_title: Should ReliableSpout extend Spout?
issue_body:
Reading through the code (looks really good by the way, we've been using `storm.py` for years, not knowing this was here :sob:) it reads as if `ReliableSpout` is expecting to inherit from `Spout` rather than `Component`? In addition to missing a `_run` loop, which I suspect is fine being identical to `Spout`'s, it also misses the stubbed out methods called from it (`_run`): `activate`, `deactivate` and `next_tuple`.

It does function fine inheriting from `Component`, since the missing methods are only called from the also missing `_run` loop, hence the questioning nature of this issue.

If its current inheritance is intentional a pointer to what the correct usage for it would be would be very appreciated, thanks! :slightly_smiling_face:

issue_title: Please use semantic versioning
issue_body:
Hello,

We're an avid user of Django Axes. We use it to guard a custom user model. 

Therefore we've implemented Django Axes a bit differently than normal.
We were using the api in the decorators.py file.

Between 2.3.2 and 2.3.3 the external api didn't change. However the internal API 
changed significantly. Queue to us having complains from users that they can't login anymore.
Specifically the way the cache key was set up tripped us over.

So to go forward: 
- Please include a more detailed changelog
- Please switch to semantic versioning with this needing a major version bump.

This seems related:
https://github.com/jazzband/django-axes/issues/246

Regards,

Roland

tldr: Use semantic versioning.

issue_title: Parsing event file spec in Windows
issue_body: In `backend.application.parse_event_files_spec` a log directory 'spec' is partitioned with respect to `:` (line 267) to allow the `run_name:path` expression. However, this handicaps Windows user with log directories residing on other drives, e.g. `D:\` as the path is split (without using the `run_name` exp). using `os.path.splitdrive` can solve this, but only if we limit the `run_name` to be longer than 1 letter.

issue_title: PyKeyboardEvent-- keyboard events still captured after stop method is called
issue_body:
Hello, I am using Linux Mint 18.

I have tried out the `capture=True` option for the PyMouseEvent and PyKeyboardEvent classes. Test script:

```
import pykeyboard, pymouse
import time, threading

hook_keyb=pykeyboard.PyKeyboardEvent(capture=True)
hook_mouse=pymouse.PyMouseEvent(capture=True)

def f(*args,**kwargs):
    print(args,kwargs)
hook_keyb.tap=f
hook_mouse.click=f

def run_hook(hook):
    print(hook," running")
    hook.run()
    print(hook," not running")
for hook in (hook_mouse,hook_keyb):
    Run_Thread=threading.Thread(target=run_hook,args=(hook,))
    Run_Thread.start()
    time.sleep(10)
    hook.stop()

time.sleep(10)
print("script finished")
```

What happens:
- In the first 10s, all pressed mouse buttons are captured and displayed, everything fine.
- In the second 10s, all pressed keyboard buttons are captured and displayed, while mouse buttons are not captured. Everything fine.
- In the last 10s before the script is finished, the keyhook is over and  nothing is displayed anymore. However, the script is still capturing all my key presses and I can't type :-(
- The key capturing stops only when the script is finally exiting at the end.

Is this a bug or due to lack of implementation?

issue_body: We want to make the standalones in Python 3.  I need to figure out if there are any differences with doing this in Python 2 vs. Python 3.

issue_title: Python 3: RuntimeError al intentar leer un catálogo XLSX
issue_body:
En Python 3, al intentar importar el catálogo de IGN, se produce un `RuntimeError`:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/federico/Workspace/datos/pydatajson/pydatajson/core.py", line 75, in __init__
    default_values=default_values)
  File "/home/federico/Workspace/datos/pydatajson/pydatajson/readers.py", line 84, in read_catalog
    catalog_dict = read_xlsx_catalog(catalog)
  File "/home/federico/Workspace/datos/pydatajson/pydatajson/readers.py", line 229, in read_xlsx_catalog
    catalog_dict = read_local_xlsx_catalog(tmpfilename, logger)
  File "/home/federico/Workspace/datos/pydatajson/pydatajson/readers.py", line 478, in read_local_xlsx_catalog
    for old_key in theme.keys():
RuntimeError: dictionary changed size during iteration
```

El problema podría estar relacionado al método `keys()` de `dict`, que en Python 2 realiza una copia de los keys, pero en Python 3 devuelve un iterador que utiliza los valores actuales del diccionario.

issue_title: BundleOptions should be a field of CallOptions, not ApiCallDefaults
sequence_length in tflearn.lstm(net, 128, sequence_length=?)
issue_body:
Hi TFlearn team,

I want to specify the sequence length in lstm for input sequence with dynamic length.

In the lstm layer in tflearn, it says:

"sequence_length: Specifies the length of each sequence in inputs. An int32 or int64 vector (tensor) size `[batch_size]`."

link:https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/tflearn/layers/recurrent.py

And I have a model like this: model.fit(trainX, trainY, n_epoch=2000, shuffle=None, validation_set=(testX, testY), batch_size=1024) 

How can specify the sequence length for both trainX and testX?

For example: my trainX is in form [1000, 300, 150], which has 1000 samples, for each samples there are max 300 timesteps (mostly the latter half is zero), for each time step the input dimension is 150.

The same goes for testX.

Then what will be my sequence_length looks like? A list of 2000 length with int numbers, which specify the number of time steps (sequence length)? Or a two dimentional nparray, which has first column as index and second is the sequnce length?

By the way is there any examples about this sequence length?

When will DNN class support sequence length?

Thank you!

Best,
Rui
